perturbation.original_text: 2. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 2.1 As used in the Contract, the following terms have the meanings indicated: "Buyer" means STASCO in its capacity as Buyer under the Offtake Agreement and any other person in its capacity as buyer under any Other Agreement. "Commodity" is defined in item 1.1.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 2.1 As used in the Contract, the following terms have the meanings indicated: "Buyer" means STASCO exclusively in its capacity as Buyer under the Offtake Agreement. "Commodity" is defined in item 1.1.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Buyer" has been narrowed to mean only STASCO under the Offtake Agreement, removing 'any other person in its capacity as buyer under any Other Agreement.' This contradicts section 4.2 (ii) where the Principal is required to independently conclude contracts with the Buyer on selling Crude Oil for export and submit a copy of each such contract with the Buyer to the Company, therefore, implying that there may be other buyers than STASCO, thus creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 At all times during the term of this Contract, the Principal shall: (i) Deliver Karakuduk Commodity to the Company at Metering Point 719 (PSP Samara of the Western branch office of KazTransOil) in the amount determined in accordance with item 1.2 of this Agreement; (ii) independently conclude contracts with the Buyer on selling Crude Oil for export and submit a copy of each such contract with the Buyer to the Company 5 days before the beginning of the relevant Delivery Month; (iii) obtain, at its own expense, required official export permissions (certificate of origin of the Commodity at place of the load output, customs declaration on Commodity output) for release of the batch of Commodity being delivered from the customs territory of the Republic of Kazakstan and deliver them before the 5th day of the Delivery Month; (iv) if the Delivery Basis is DAF, provide the Company before the 5th day of the Delivery Month with a copy of confirmation from AK Transneft on its readiness to accept the agreed batch of Commodity from coordinators at the relevant directions; 4 (v) if the Delivery Basis is FOB, to provide the Company and the Company's agent in the relevant port 5 days before the agreed upon loading&bbsp;period (Laycan) with the Buyer's instructions on filling in the shipping documents, including name of the vessel, its characteristics, loading period (Laycan), lay time and other necessary data; (vi) make a 100% pre-payment for transportation of the batch of Commodity through the territory of Russian Federation and the CIS countries, in accordance with the Payment Order of AK Transneft; (vii) pay the additional expenses if the transport tariffs are increased, providing the Company presents the appropriate documents; (viii) reimburse the Company for the cost of execution of the customs declaration at the Energy customs office of the RF in accordance with the Company's invoice; (ix) reimburse the Company for all expenses reasonably incurred in connection with the performance by the Company of its obligations under this Contract within 30 (thirty) days of delivery to the Principal of proper invoices and other supporting documents for such expenses; (x) pay the Company's fee for the services rendered under Article 5 hereof, in accordance with the invoice and tax invoice; (xi) provide copies of all payment documents; (xii) pay all transportation and other expense of KazTransOil on the territory of Kazakhstan under its contract with KazTransOil; (xiii) to make all necessary payments to the budget of the Republic of Kazakhstan related to excise taxes, VAT and other obligatory payments to the budget in accordance with the tax legislation (xiv) during the term of this Contract, observe all provisions of this Contract and other terms and conditions agreed upon with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Buyer' to exclusively mean STASCO under the Offtake Agreement, removing the possibility of 'any other person in its capacity as buyer under any Other Agreement'. This directly contradicts section 4.2 (ii), which states that the Principal must 'independently conclude contracts with the Buyer on selling Crude Oil for export and submit a copy of each such contract with the Buyer to the Company', implying there could be multiple buyers, not just STASCO. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the modified definition in Section 2 makes the obligation in Section 4.2(ii) regarding contracts with 'the Buyer' ambiguous and potentially unenforceable if there are other buyers than STASCO, as it is not clear that such other contracts would still fall under the definition in the modified text.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Unless otherwise agreed by the Parties, the Principal shall at all times deliver to the Company Karakuduk Commodity pursuant to this Contract. The Company shall ensure that (i) during the Principal Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the relevant delivery point shall be REBCO, and (ii) during the Secondary Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the CPC Terminal shall be Karakuduk Commodity for segregated batch deliveries or shall be CPC Blend for deliveries for which segregated batch delivery is not available.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Unless otherwise agreed by the Parties, the Principal should attempt to deliver to the Company Karakuduk Commodity pursuant to this Contract. The Company shall ensure that (i) during the Principal Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the relevant delivery point shall be REBCO, and (ii) during the Secondary Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the CPC Terminal shall be any available Commodity, regardless of quality, for deliveries for which segregated batch delivery is not available.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'should attempt to' makes the Principal's obligation to deliver Karakuduk Commodity less binding. Changing 'CPC Blend' to 'any available Commodity, regardless of quality' during the Secondary Period introduces a contradiction. This is because, the original definition establishes specific quality standards for the commodity to be delivered, while the modified version allows for any quality, potentially conflicting with the implied quality standards throughout the contract, especially in section 8 where it states the Parties shall be liable for non-execution and/or improper execution of their obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. LIABILITY; INDEMNITY 8.1 The Parties shall be liable for non-execution and/or improper execution of their obligations under this Agreement in accordance with the legislation of the Republic of Kazakstan. 8.2 In case if the Principal violates the conditions of this Contract, and such violation entails infringement of the Off-take Agreement conditions by the Principal, then the Principal shall be solely liable in front of the Purchaser for such violations. In addition, the Company shall not be liable in front of the Principal or the Purchaser hereunder. 8.3 In case if violation by the Principal of the Off-take Agreement conditions takes place due to infringement by the Company of its liabilities in front of the Principal under this Contract, the Company shall be soley liable in front of the Principal for such losses resulting from such violation , evidenced and supported by confirming documents ..
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the Principal's obligation to deliver Karakuduk Commodity by changing 'shall' to 'should attempt to'. Furthermore, it introduces the possibility of delivering 'any available Commodity, regardless of quality' during the Secondary Period. This directly contradicts section 8.1, which states that 'The Parties shall be liable for non-execution and/or improper execution of their obligations under this Agreement'. By allowing for delivery of any quality commodity, the changed text creates ambiguity about what constitutes 'improper execution', thus contradicting the liability clause and creating uncertainty regarding enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 In accordance with items 9.3, 9.4, and 10.3, this Contract shall come into force on the Effective Date, remain effective throughout the Initial Term, and be prolonged, or further extended automatically for a period of 12 months, each such extension commencing at the end of the last day of the Initial Term or the relevant anniversary thereof, unless either Party serves written notice of termination on the other Party at least 65 days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any subsequent extension.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 In accordance with items 9.3, 9.4, and 10.3, this Contract shall come into force on the Effective Date, remain effective throughout the Initial Term, and be prolonged, or further extended automatically for a period of 12 months, each such extension commencing at the end of the last day of the Initial Term or the relevant anniversary thereof, unless either Party serves written notice of termination on the other Party at least 65 days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any subsequent extension. However, the contract can be terminated at any time with mutual agreement of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, the contract can be terminated at any time with mutual agreement of both parties' creates a contradiction with the original clause that outlines a specific process for termination (65 days notice). This creates ambiguity regarding the termination process, creating an in-text contradiction as, either the contract is only terminated by giving a 65 day notice, or it can be terminated anytime when both parties agree to do so. This contradicts 9.2 which states Any notice of termination served by any Party with violation of the period of notice required by item 9.1 shall be invalid and of no effect whatsoever, because now the clause has been changed to include that if both parties agree, then the contract can be terminated
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2 Any notice of termination served by any Party with violation of the period of notice required by item 9.1 shall be invalid and of no effect whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in 9.1 introduces a contradiction by stating that the contract can be terminated at any time with mutual agreement, conflicting with 9.2, which renders any termination notice invalid if it violates the 65-day notice period specified in the original 9.1. This creates a direct conflict regarding the termination process, as one section demands a 65-day notice, while the modified section allows immediate termination upon mutual agreement.
